steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The phrase though it seemed sets up a contrast between the initial perception (blank) and the actual outcome (eventually solved).

steps.title: Match the contrast
steps.content:
Plug each choice into the blank: "_____ though it seemed, the conjecture eventually yielded."

A. Insuperable means impossible to overcome. "Insuperable though it seemed" contrasts perfectly with "eventually yielded"—the conjecture looked unsolvable but was solved. Correct.

B. Unequivocal means absolutely clear. A clear conjecture that eventually gets solved is not a meaningful contrast—nothing was surprising about that. Eliminated.

C. Irreproachable means beyond criticism or faultless. A faultless conjecture yielding to proof makes no logical contrast. Eliminated.

D. Ineluctable means inevitable. If the conjecture seemed inevitable, its eventual solution would not be a contrast—it would be expected. Eliminated.

A is correct.
